stem: In the xy-plane, line q has a slope of -7/4 and an x-intercept of (-8, 0). What is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of line q?
rationale: The correct answer is -14. A line can be written as y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. Given m = -7/4 and the point (-8, 0): 0 = (-7/4)(-8) + b = 14 + b, so b = -14.
